scenario: Echo Bank evaluates KYC outcomes on Camunda 8.8 across 13000 transactions per day and stores per-check booleans in a process variable list such as [true, false]. A modeller writes an exclusive gateway condition that returns false when any element of the list is false and returns true on an empty list, using the documented FEEL function and replacement note on the list built-in functions page.
question: Which FEEL list function returns false when any list element is false and which legacy function did it replace per the docs?

options.text: all(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns false if any element of the list is false, returns true on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function and() per the conversion docs note on the list built-in functions page

every(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns false if any element of the list is false, returns true on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function and() per the conversion docs note on the list built-in functions page

and(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns false if any element of the list is false, returns true on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and is the current FEEL conjunction function on the list built-in functions page on Camunda 8.8

fold and(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns false if any element of the list is false, returns true on an empty list, accepts the list as a sequence of elements, and replaced the previous function and() per the conversion docs note on the list built-in functions page

a.text: Correct. The list built-in functions page documents all(list: list<boolean>): boolean. The function returns false if any element of the list is false, returns true on an empty list, and the list parameter can be passed as a list or as a sequence of elements. The page notes that all() replaced the previous and() function, which is now the previous Camunda Extension function.

b.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page does not document an every() function; the documented all-true-or-empty function is all(list).

c.text: Incorrect. The docs state that and() was replaced by all() and that the previous function should not be used anymore; and() is not the current FEEL conjunction function on the list built-in functions page.

d.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page does not document a fold and() function; the documented all-true-or-empty function is all(list).

explanation: all(list: list<boolean>): boolean returns false if any element of the list is false and returns true otherwise, including on an empty list. The list parameter can be passed as a list or as a sequence of elements. The page notes that all() replaced the previous and() function, which should not be used anymore.
